text.translated_captions:
WEBVTT

1
00:00:00.240 --> 00:00:03.510
As a front-end developer,
like in any technical job,

2
00:00:03.710 --> 00:00:05.100
you need the right tools.

3
00:00:05.640 --> 00:00:09.870
In this course, I’ll guide you
through the installation and setup,

4
00:00:10.070 --> 00:00:12.750
and help you discover three
essential tools.

5
00:00:13.440 --> 00:00:17.730
First, Visual Studio Code, which will be
your main tool for writing code,

6
00:00:17.930 --> 00:00:21.540
with its advanced editor,
file explorer,

7
00:00:21.740 --> 00:00:24.600
command palette, and all its
built-in tooling.

8
00:00:25.410 --> 00:00:30.060
Then we’ll look at Chrome and Firefox,
two must-have browsers

9
00:00:30.260 --> 00:00:34.020
for analyzing and testing your projects,
thanks to their powerful developer

10
00:00:34.220 --> 00:00:34.980
tools.

11
00:00:35.180 --> 00:00:39.450
By the end of this course, you’ll be able
to set up your front-end environment.

12
00:00:39.650 --> 00:00:40.410

13
00:00:40.610 --> 00:00:42.060
So, ready to get started ?

14
00:00:42.260 --> 00:00:43.770
Join me in the first
chapter.
text.editors_notes:
Production notes
- Format: WebVTT preserved; timecodes unchanged.
- Cue 9–10: Kept the line break across cues for timing; avoids cramming “developer tools” into cue 9.
- Cue 12 is empty in the source; left empty to preserve structure (do not remove).
- Potential sync risk (slightly dense): Cue 5 (00:00:13.440–00:00:17.730) lists function + purpose; kept concise.
- HTML entity preserved: “ ” kept exactly (cue 13).

Terminology decisions
- “développeur front-end” → “front-end developer” (consistent).
- “palette de commandes” → “command palette” (standard VS Code term).
- “outillage intégré” → “built-in tooling” (natural spoken EN).

🎙️ Pronunciation & Phonetics Guide
| Term | Cue timecode(s) | Strategy | Pronunciation hint (EN) | Syllable impact | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| front-end | 00:00:00.240–00:00:03.510; 00:00:35.180–00:00:39.450 | Keep as-is | “front end” | 0 | TTS may hyphenate oddly; most engines handle it fine. |
| Visual Studio Code | 00:00:13.440–00:00:17.730 | Keep as-is | “VIZH-oo-uhl STOO-dee-oh koad” | 0 | If the chosen voice rushes, consider “VS Code” in audio script (not in subtitles unless requested). |
| VS Code | (not used) | Optional alternate | “V-S Code” | -3 vs full name | Shorter for dubbing; can help lip-sync if needed. |
| Chrome | 00:00:25.410–00:00:30.060 | Keep as-is | “Krohm” | 0 | Usually safe. |
| Firefox | 00:00:25.410–00:00:30.060 | Keep as-is | “FY-er-fox” | 0 | Usually safe. |
| command palette | 00:00:21.740–00:00:24.600 | Keep as-is | “kuh-MAND PAL-it” | 0 | Standard dev term; clear stress helps TTS. |

content.text:
### 🏁 1) Audit Overview

- **Direction:** French → US English  
- **Domain:** Tech / Front-end tooling onboarding (course intro, subtitles)  
- **Localizability score:** **92/100**  
- **Penalties (examples):**
  - −4 Minor AV/formatting cleanup (HTML entity ` ` in VTT)
  - −2 Potential UI-language bias (VS Code / Chrome / Firefox menus may be shown in French in the video)
  - −2 Pedagogical header/CTA could be more action-oriented for US tone (minor rewrite)

---

### 🌍 2) Deep Cultural & Societal Adaptation Report

json.editors_notes:
Production notes
- Format: WebVTT preserved; timecodes unchanged.
- Cue 9–10: Kept the line break across cues for timing; avoids cramming “developer tools” into cue 9.
- Cue 12 is empty in the source; left empty to preserve structure (do not remove).
- Potential sync risk (slightly dense): Cue 5 (00:00:13.440–00:00:17.730) lists function + purpose; kept concise.
- HTML entity preserved: “ ” kept exactly (cue 13).

Terminology decisions
- “développeur front-end” → “front-end developer” (consistent).
- “palette de commandes” → “command palette” (standard VS Code term).
- “outillage intégré” → “built-in tooling” (natural spoken EN).

🎙️ Pronunciation & Phonetics Guide
| Term | Cue timecode(s) | Strategy | Pronunciation hint (EN) | Syllable impact | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| front-end | 00:00:00.240–00:00:03.510; 00:00:35.180–00:00:39.450 | Keep as-is | “front end” | 0 | TTS may hyphenate oddly; most engines handle it fine. |
| Visual Studio Code | 00:00:13.440–00:00:17.730 | Keep as-is | “VIZH-oo-uhl STOO-dee-oh koad” | 0 | If the chosen voice rushes, consider “VS Code” in audio script (not in subtitles unless requested). |
| VS Code | (not used) | Optional alternate | “V-S Code” | -3 vs full name | Shorter for dubbing; can help lip-sync if needed. |
| Chrome | 00:00:25.410–00:00:30.060 | Keep as-is | “Krohm” | 0 | Usually safe. |
| Firefox | 00:00:25.410–00:00:30.060 | Keep as-is | “FY-er-fox” | 0 | Usually safe. |
| command palette | 00:00:21.740–00:00:24.600 | Keep as-is | “kuh-MAND PAL-it” | 0 | Standard dev term; clear stress helps TTS. |
